props.title: Formengine Core Open-Source, Free JSON Form Renderer React Library
props.description: From simple to complex React forms — built with minimal code, no deep React knowledge, and without compromising performance

props.title: WorkflowEngine Core Free React Form Library Architecture
props.description: This is the main Form Builder package that contains the React methods and components for displaying the form. This package must be used at runtime.

props.title: The Most Flexible Open Source Library for React Forms

features.title: Open Source
features.text: Gives you full control, transparency, and flexibility. You can audit the code, adapt it to your needs, and rely on a community-backed project with no vendor lock-in or hidden limitations.

features.title: MIT License
features.text: Free to use, modify, and embed in commercial projects. No restrictions or lock-in. Ideal for integrating into your React apps or building your own solution on top.

features.title: Easy custom component integration
features.text: Quickly define your own component by specifying its name, type, and props.

features.title: Ready to use with popular frameworks
features.text: Works seamlessly with Next.js, Remix, and other modern React stacks with no special setup required.

features.title: Ready-to-use component library
features.text: Includes built-in components and supports easy integration of custom ones. Get started fast without sacrificing flexibility.

features.title: Reusable forms across apps
features.text: Just copy and use. Forms are easily portable between applications with minimal effort. A single JSON object contains the full structure, logic, styles, validation, and actions.

features.title: Built-in actions
features.text: Forms support actions like submit, reset, and external calls out of the box. Define only what you need in code or JSON — and it'll just work.

features.title: Built-in form validation
features.text: Data validation works out of the box with no extra code. Required fields, error messages, and rules are defined directly in code or JSON. Easily integrates with third-party validation libraries.

features.title: Built-in localization
features.text: Forms are ready for multi-language support out of the box. Third-party localization libraries can be integrated if needed.

features.title: Dynamic CSS and responsive layout support
features.text: Forms automatically adapt to different devices and styles — no additional layout code needed.

features.title: Programmatic form creation and dynamic generation
features.text: Build or modify forms in real time using code or external data sources.

features.title: Lower learning curve
features.text: Easily create forms using typed WorkflowEngine Core functions, without writing components or learning complex React concepts. JSON representation of the form is also supported for configuration-driven use.

props.title: Build a Working React Form in Minutes
props.subtitle: No advanced React skills required
props.description: This short video shows how to create a fully functional form in React using WorkflowEngine Core. You define your form as JSON, and the library handles layout, validation, and logic for you. It's perfect for beginners, small projects, or teams looking for a quick and reliable way to build forms without writing boilerplate code.

props.title: Build forms faster for a fraction of the cost
props.description: Quickly build any internal app, automate any workflow and schedule any job. Save precious engineering time so you can focus on your next big product launch.

props.title: Build complex React forms programmatically using a fluent API
props.subtitle: Programmatic Form Creation
props.description: This advanced example shows how to build a dynamic form programmatically using the buildForm() function and Rsuite UI components. The form includes layout RsContainer, inputs RsInput, a date picker, validation rules, and a custom submit action.

props.subtitle: Custom components
props.title: Easy custom component integration
props.description: Easily integrate custom components by specifying their name, type, and props. Add custom fields, groups, wrappers, or dynamic behaviors — and match your design system and business logic requirements.

props.subtitle: JSON Form Renderer for Next.js and Modern React
props.title: Effortless integration with Next.js, Remix, and other React frameworks
props.description: Render dynamic forms in your Next.js app with just one JSON file. WorkflowEngine handles layout, logic, and validation out of the box — fully customizable, MIT-licensed, and production-ready.
props.secondDescription: You can find fully functional examples in our GitHub under the examples folder. Explore the code to see how the components work together. Need help? Just reach out!

tabs.name: Next.js
tabs.code:
git clone git@github.com:optimajet/workflowengine.git
cd workflowengine/premium/examples/with-nextjs/with-nextjs-v15
npm install
npm run dev

tabs.name: Remix
tabs.code:
git clone git@github.com:optimajet/workflowengine.git
cd workflowengine/premium/examples/with-remix/with-remix-v2
npm install
npm run dev

props.subtitle: Say goodbye to the hassle of searching for elements on GitHub and struggling to make them work.
props.title: Ready-to-use components library
props.description: Includes built-in components and supports easy integration of custom ones. Get started fast without sacrificing flexibility.
props.secondDescription: With WorkflowEngine, you get a comprehensive package of ready-to-use components that can be easily customized to meet your specific needs.

props.title: Customize the appearance and behavior of form elements
props.subtitle: Customization Options
props.description: Apply component styling, and add your own validation rules or data processing logic. A flexible and customizable form builder can adapt to your project's unique requirements.
props.imageAlt: Form customization examples with different themes

props.subtitle: Built-in Functionality
props.title: Built-in actions
props.description: Forms support actions like submit, reset, and external calls out of the box. Define only what you need in code or JSON — and it'll just work.

props.code:
<FormBuilder
  actions={{
    logEventArgs: e => console.log(e),
    assertArgs: ActionDefinition.functionalAction((e, args) => {
      console.log(e, args)
    }, {
      p1: 'string',
      p2: 'boolean',
      p3: 'number'
    })
  }}
/>

props.subtitle: Native and Third-Party Form Validation
props.title: Built-in form validation
props.description: WorkflowEngine Core includes built-in validation for required fields, error messages, and custom rules. You can define everything directly in JSON or code. It also integrates easily with popular validation libraries such as Yup, AJV, Zod, Superstruct, Joi, or your own custom logic. No extra setup is needed.

props.subtitle: Internationalization
props.title: Built-in localization
props.description: Forms are ready for multi-language support out of the box. Third-party localization libraries can be integrated if needed.

features.title: Multilingual Input Values
features.description: Support for multiple languages in form input values and user data.

features.title: Localizable Form Builder UI
features.description: The form builder interface itself can be translated to different languages.

features.title: Localizable Form UI
features.description: All form elements, labels, validation messages, and configuration properties can be localized.

props.subtitle: Adaptive layout
props.title: Dynamic CSS and responsive layout support
props.description: Forms automatically adapt to different devices and styles — no additional layout code needed.

image.alt: Adaptive layout demonstration with desktop and mobile views

props.subtitle: Dynamic and interactive forms that adapt to user choices or input
props.title: Conditional Logic and Dynamic Form
props.description: Define rules or conditions that control the visibility or behavior of form fields or sections based on specific criteria or user input. With FormBuilder conditional logic, you can set up rules that determine when certain form elements should be displayed, hidden, or disabled, based on the values or selections made in other fields.
props.secondDescription: With Optimajet FormBuilder, you typically define conditional logic through a visual interface or configuration settings. This allows you to specify the conditions and actions associated with each form field or section. The FormBuilder then handles the logic and updates the form's state or DOM dynamically based on the defined conditions.

props.title: FAQ WorkflowEngine Core React Form Library

faqItems.question: What is Optimajet WorkflowEngine Core?
faqItems.answer: WorkflowEngine Core is a high-performance headless form engine that renders dynamic forms from JSON and lets you fully control form logic from the backend.

faqItems.question: How is WorkflowEngine Core different from React Hook Form or Formik?
faqItems.answer: Unlike React Hook Form or Formik, WorkflowEngine allows you to define entire forms in JSON — including structure, logic, validation, and actions — making it ideal for backend-driven applications and dynamic UIs.

faqItems.question: Does WorkflowEngine Core work with Next.js or Remix?
faqItems.answer: Yes. It integrates seamlessly with modern React frameworks like Next.js and Remix — no special setup required.

faqItems.question: Can I control form logic from the backend?
faqItems.answer: Absolutely. WorkflowEngine was built for server-side control. You can modify fields, validation, conditions, and visibility entirely from your backend.

faqItems.question: Does it support custom React components?
faqItems.answer: Yes. You can plug in your own custom components by simply specifying the type and props in JSON or through a builder interface.

faqItems.question: Is dynamic logic and conditional rendering supported?
faqItems.answer: Yes. WorkflowEngine supports reactive field logic — such as show/hide, dynamic validation, conditional actions — all declaratively defined.

faqItems.question: Is WorkflowEngine Core open-source?
faqItems.answer: Yes. WorkflowEngine Core is fully open-source and released under the MIT license. It's free to use, modify, and embed in commercial or private projects — no limitations, no vendor lock-in.

faqItems.question: Can I reuse the same form in multiple projects?
faqItems.answer: Yes. Forms are fully portable between apps. The entire form — structure, styling, validation, and logic — lives in a single JSON object you can copy and reuse.

faqItems.question: Is WorkflowEngine Core fast and lightweight?
faqItems.answer: Yes. It's designed for performance — minimal re-renders, no heavy runtime, and scalable even with large and complex forms.

faqItems.question: Can WorkflowEngine Core be used without a visual builder?
faqItems.answer: Yes. You can define and manage forms entirely via JSON or programmatically through the typed API — no need for a visual interface unless you want one.

faqItems.question: Does WorkflowEngine Core support form validation?
faqItems.answer: Yes. Validation rules are defined declaratively in JSON. You can use built-in rules (like required, min/max) or define custom validation logic.

faqItems.question: Can forms be updated dynamically at runtime?
faqItems.answer: Yes. WorkflowEngine supports runtime changes to fields, values, validation, and visibility — ideal for dynamic UIs driven by external data or user input.

faqItems.question: Does it support multi-step forms or form wizards?
faqItems.answer: Yes. You can define multi-step workflows and control navigation between steps through schema configuration or backend logic.

faqItems.question: Can I use WorkflowEngine Core in microservice-based systems?
faqItems.answer: Absolutely. WorkflowEngine is ideal for distributed architectures where form definitions and logic are managed by backend services and rendered on the frontend.

faqItems.question: How does WorkflowEngine Core handle form actions like submit or reset?
faqItems.answer: Form actions are built-in and configurable. You can define submit, reset, external API calls, or custom handlers declaratively within the schema.

faqItems.question: Is WorkflowEngine Core suitable for enterprise applications?
faqItems.answer: Yes. It's built with flexibility, performance, and backend control in mind — making it perfect for B2B SaaS, internal platforms, and complex enterprise forms.

faqItems.question: Can I integrate WorkflowEngine Core with my existing design system?
faqItems.answer: Yes. Since WorkflowEngine is headless, you can map form elements to your own styled React components and preserve your design system.

faqItems.question: Does WorkflowEngine Core support internationalization (i18n)?
faqItems.answer: Yes. Labels, placeholders, messages, and validation errors can all be defined in a way that supports localization and language switching.

faqItems.question: Can I preload form data or populate fields with external values?
faqItems.answer: Yes. You can hydrate forms with initial values from your backend or external API and update field values reactively as needed.

faqItems.question: Is there a community or support available?
faqItems.answer: Yes. You can join the growing WorkflowEngine community on GitHub, access the official documentation, or contact the team for commercial support options.

testimonials.text: OptimaJet's FormBuilder has been a game-changer for our form creation process. Our end users are delighted with the ability to customize the form's layout and structure without any coding knowledge. It has truly streamlined our workflow and empowered our team to create visually appealing and user-friendly forms.
testimonials.name: Samantha Lee
testimonials.title: Staff Software Engineer

testimonials.text: We've been able to produce forms two times faster than we were before FormBuilder. Adding FormBuilder to the scope was definitely the right thing for us. As a developer, I appreciate the ability to apply custom CSS styling, add external validators, and implement personalized data processing logic. The FormBuilder adapts perfectly to our project's unique requirements.
testimonials.name: Milovan Popovic
testimonials.title: Enterprise Applications Manager

testimonials.text: FormBuilder has empowered us to create a truly tailored and seamless form-building experience. The best part? You can easily tweak and customize them to match our exact needs. The ability to monitor other fields and run validations asynchronously adds a whole new level of flexibility.

testimonials.title: CTO
